pi_items.source: ImageJ official documentation and direct download from https://imagej.net/images/blobs.gif

pi_items.verified_value: Otsu thresholding (threshold value: 120 for the Blobs image)
pi_items.source: Verified by applying Otsu's method to the Blobs image; confirmed as standard ImageJ thresholding method at https://imagej.net/ij/docs/menus/analyze.html
Task specification and verified by filtering Blobs image particles with area >= 60 pixels
Task specification and verified by classifying filtered particles with cutoff at 350 pixels

Direct analysis of Blobs (25K) image downloaded from https://imagej.net/images/blobs.gif. Image dimensions: 256x254 pixels. After Otsu thresholding (threshold=120), noise filtering (>=60px), found 25 particles in range [60-350]px, which falls within expected range [20-50]
Direct analysis of Blobs (25K) image. After Otsu thresholding and classification at 350px cutoff, found 32 particles > 350px, which falls within expected range [10-40]
Task description in task.json specifies these output files are required

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the Blobs (25K) sample image from the official ImageJ repository (256x254 pixels, ~25KB, available at https://imagej.net/images/blobs.gif). This is a real, widely-used public dataset, not synthetic. When analyzed with Otsu thresholding (automatic threshold value: 120), the image yields 64 total particles. After noise filtering (removing particles <60 pixels), 57 particles remain. These are correctly classified into 25 small particles (60-350px) and 32 large particles (>350px), both falling within the expected ranges [20-50] and [10-40] respectively. The threshold method 'Default/Otsu' is verified as the standard automatic thresholding method in ImageJ. All metadata claims about particle counts and image properties are consistent with empirical analysis of the actual image data.
